Now this thing here guys, just so you know, what Facebook does here, when you're running a conversion based campaign, optimizing for conversions, you're essentially using ECPM, effective cost per thousand impressions. Now part of the calculation for ECPM, it takes into account bid, relevance, click-through rate, and conversion rate. So conversion rate is how many people is Facebook sending to you versus how many people are triggering the event that you're tracking for, right? Or optimizing for. And that's how it calculates, is this funnel actually working, or am I just sending leads into a dud atmosphere or a dud funnel? So what it does is, essentially if your conversion rates are really low, Facebook recommends that you're using link clicks first to build up that data. And that's what we used to do about three, four months ago when you launched conversion campaigns. You used to optimize directly for link clicks to begin with. And then you would switch it to conversions

## [42:27 - 45:27]

once you have had enough conversion triggers happening, event triggers happening. Nowadays what they recommend is you actually go straight for conversions. So you just optimize your ad delivery straight for conversions. However, you need to have at least 20 event triggers per ad set per week. Meaning your conversion event should actually be whatever step in your funnel that's at least getting 20 of those triggers. Because if you have less, it impacts that conversion rate part of the algorithm. And Facebook goes, this isn't a good funnel. So it costs you more money and you lose the auction process. And because most people don't understand that or they don't understand how that works, or they don't understand to optimize for the step before, that's why they've rolled this out. For people that are not quite understanding that and Facebook just goes, okay, I'll just figure this out and I'll do link clicks until I feel like you're ready for conversions. So because you're doing, essentially at the moment you're doing lead, which is like an opt-in, I suspect you would be getting those opt-ins once everything tracks properly.
